name: Wilderland
description: The open lands of Rhovanion east of the Misty Mountains — the Anduin Vales, the plains north of Mirkwood, the Carrock country. A continental climate: hotter summers, colder winters, weather that arrives with purpose.

spring.label: Fair and cool, a high clean sky, the grass bright after the thaw.
Running showers and sunshine, the Anduin full and brown.
A hard easterly wind off the plains, clouds driven fast.
A spring thunderstorm rolling down from the mountains — brief and loud.
River-mist thick in the valleys until mid-morning.
Grey overcast and a damp chill — the spring slow in coming.
Unseasonably warm and still — the first true day of spring.
A last wet snowfall, melting before it can settle.

summer.label: Hot and clear, the sun fierce by noon, little shade on the open ground.
Warm with a steady breeze — good travelling weather.
Building heat all day, breaking into thunder and hard rain by evening.
Hazy and oppressively still — the horizon lost in heat-shimmer.
A grey steady rain from dawn to dusk, unusual for the season.
A dry wind off the eastern plains, dust in the air.
A violent thunderstorm — lightning walking on the horizon, travellers take cover.
A cool bright day between the heat — grateful weather.

autumn.label: Crisp and fair, the Anduin Vales gold and russet under a clean sky.
A cold autumn rain, the sort that hurries travellers toward hearths.
Heavy mist lying on the river and plain until the sun climbs high.
A bitter wind from the east, leaves stripped in hours.
Flat grey overcast, a damp chill, the light failing early.
An autumn storm — driving rain and branches down across the road.
The first hard frost, puddles iced, breath steaming.
An early fall of wet snow, unsettling and half-melted by afternoon.

winter.label: Clear and bitterly cold, the sky hard and blue, the shadows long.
A steady fall of dry snow, the plain turning white to the horizon.
Leaden overcast, a raw wind, no warmth in the day at all.
A true blizzard off the east — wind, driving snow, travel foolhardy.
Freezing fog, hoarfrost on every surface, the world reduced to a few paces.
Sleet and half-snow, the roads treacherous underfoot.
A still, cold day — the silence of deep winter on the land.
A cold that kills the careless — ink freezes, metal burns bare skin.
